title: Meta Platforms (META) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 81/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: 16% growth acknowledges Meta’s undeniable operating leverage. With over 3 billion daily active users across the Family of Apps, the marginal cost of deploying new AI-driven ad features is near zero. Even as Reality Labs burns billions, the core business generates such massive cash flows that a 16% CAGR is highly attainable as AI efficiencies compound.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: <div class="assumption-grid" data-astro-cid-4rs3crxi> <div class="assumption-card" data-astro-cid-4rs3crxi> <div class="card-title" data-astro-cid-4rs3crxi>FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.5% implies Meta will grow slightly faster than global GDP into perpetuity. In an AI-augmented world where communication and attention are paramount, Meta controls the ultimate distribution channels. Its infrastructure serves as a global economic utility.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Why I'm Bullish on the Efficiency Engine
thesisParagraphs: Meta Platforms is no longer just a social media company. It is a massive, hyper-efficient AI advertising engine that happens to interface through apps. The narrative of 2022—burning cash on the Metaverse—has completely flipped. Meta’s massive CapEx spending on Nvidia GPUs (Llama infrastructure) is showing immediate, powerful returns. The algorithm for Reels and the core feed is driving unparalleled engagement, while automated ad suites (Advantage+) are dramatically lowering acquisition costs for advertisers.

My valuation model recognizes this dual engine: Meta is simultaneously securing its dominance as the premier global ad platform using AI while investing deeply into the next compute paradigm (Reality Labs). This creates an incredibly wide moat and justifies strong free cash flow expansion.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project a 16% Free Cash Flow growth rate for Meta?
faq.answer: Gemini projects 16% growth because Meta is already demonstrating massive ROI from its open-source AI models (Llama). AI integration is dramatically improving ad targeting efficiency and user engagement on Reels, expanding core margins even as Reality Labs investments continue.

faq.question: What discount rate (WACC) was used in Meta's DCF analysis?
faq.answer: A 9.2% discount rate was used. This reflects the 4.18% risk-free rate and Meta's slightly elevated beta compared to some big tech peers, while acknowledging its unparalleled cash-generation capabilities and global user base.

faq.question: How does Meta's Metaverse investment impact its intrinsic value?
faq.answer: The massive CapEx for Reality Labs is already priced into the base FCF of $46.1B. If these investments eventually yield a dominant mixed-reality platform, it acts as a call option on top of this valuation. If they fail but spending is curtailed, FCF margins will expand significantly faster than projected.

prospectVerdictDetail: Meta's advertising machine is the most efficient in digital media, powered by AI-driven targeting that keeps improving. The 3.2B daily active users across the family of apps create an unmatched audience. Reels monetization is closing the gap with TikTok. Reality Labs remains a $15B+ annual cash burn with uncertain returns, but the core business generates enough FCF to absorb this indefinitely.
prospectKeyRisks: Reality Labs cumulative losses exceeding $60B with no clear path to consumer-scale AR/VR adoption
Regulatory actions (EU DMA, US privacy legislation) restricting behavioral ad targeting could reduce ad effectiveness by 10-20%
TikTok maintaining its grip on Gen Z attention, permanently capturing the youngest demographic cohort
prospectKeyCatalysts: AI-driven ad targeting improvements driving 15-20% increase in advertiser ROAS
WhatsApp and Messenger monetization through business messaging reaching $5B+ ARR
Threads reaching 500M+ MAU and successfully monetizing as a Twitter/X replacement
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100.
